text: Last class you said: Could I have a window seat, please?
Today you can say: I would like a quiet room, please.
I would like a quiet room, please.
I would like to check in early, please.
Would you mind giving me a wake-up call?
Would you mind sending housekeeping up?
Good evening, and welcome to the Garden Plaza. Do you have a reservation with us?
Yes, I have a reservation under the name Tofolo. I would like a quiet room on a high floor, please.
Of course. I have room eight twelve on a high floor. May I see your passport, and we will take a small deposit on your card?
Here is my passport. I have an early meeting tomorrow. Would you mind giving me a wake-up call at six?
Not at all. A wake-up call at six. Breakfast is in the lobby, and here is your voucher. Checkout is at noon.
Thank you very much. One more thing -- would you mind sending some extra towels to my room?
I have a reservation under the name Tofolo.
What time is checkout, and what amenities do you have?

Good evening, and welcome to the Garden Plaza Hotel. This is a short message about your stay. Breakfast is served in the lobby restaurant from six thirty to ten in the morning. The fitness room and the pool are on the second floor, and they are open until eleven at night. Our amenities also include free wifi in every room and in the lobby. If you would like a wake-up call, please dial zero for the front desk, and we will be happy to arrange it. Checkout is at noon. If you need a later checkout, just let us know, and we will do our best. We hope you enjoy your stay with us. If there is anything you need, our team is here twenty-four hours a day.

When I travel abroad for a medical conference, I always book a quiet hotel near the venue. I usually arrive late and tired, so a smooth check-in really matters to me. At the front desk, I give my name and say I have a reservation. Then I ask a few simple things. I always say, I would like a room on a high floor, please, because it is quieter. And if I have an early meeting, I ask, would you mind giving me a wake-up call at six? I have learned that hotel staff are very helpful when you are polite and clear. A good night of sleep before a long conference day makes all the difference for me.

Here is what I do when I arrive at a hotel. First, I go to the front desk and say I have a reservation. Then I give my name and show my passport. After that, I leave a deposit and the staff give me my key card. Next, I ask for a wake-up call and a room on a high floor. Finally, I go up to my room and check that everything works.
I would like a quiet room, please.
I would like to check in early, please.
Would you mind giving me a wake-up call?
I have a reservation under the name Tofolo.
Would you mind sending some extra towels?
What time is checkout, and what amenities do you have?
